description_en: The grave site of Kikujo, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1989-03-25. Located at 88 Nakazato, Myogimachi.

A stone pillar inscribed with a Buddhist invocation (myogoto), classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1992-07-01. Located at 947 Nanokaichi.

name_romaji: Inabe Ichigorō Tanemasa no Haka・Kankei Monjo・Hi
name_en: Tomb, Related Documents and Monument of Inabe Ichigoro Tanemasa
description_en: The tomb, related documents and stone monument of Inabe Ichigoro Tanemasa, designated as a historic site. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1992-09-04. Located at 841, 948 and 1619 Nanokaichi.

name_romaji: Myōgi Jinja Seidōsei Tōrō Tsuketari Hōnō Monjo Oyobi Mikoshi
name_en: Bronze Lantern of Myogi Shrine with Dedication Documents and Portable Shrine
description_en: A bronze lantern of Myogi Shrine, with attached dedication documents and a portable shrine (mikoshi), classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1993-10-25. Located at 3 Myogi, Myogimachi.

name_en: Saikoji Temple Gate with Ridge Tablet and Design Drawing
description_en: The main gate (sanmon) of Saikoji, with an attached ridge tablet (munafuda) and design drawing, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1995-04-21. Located at 1133-1 Najai.
A Yakushi hall (Yakushido), classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1995-04-21. Located at 1675 Kamitakase.

name_en: Excavated Artifacts from Tomioka Burial Mound No. 5
description_en: Artifacts excavated from Tomioka burial mound No. 5, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1995-04-21. Held by the Tomioka City Board of Education, 1477-1 Tomioka.

name_romaji: Torifusa Jinja Bashō Kuhi Tsuketari Kasen Kōgyō Monjo
name_en: Basho Haiku Monument of Torifusa Shrine with Linked-Verse Documents
description_en: A haiku monument (kuhi) commemorating Basho at Torifusa Shrine, with attached linked-verse (kasen) performance documents, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1995-09-13. Located at 221 Nakazawa and the municipal art museum, 351-1 Kurokawa.
A votive mirror dedicated to Nukisaki Shrine, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1997-04-23. Located at 1535 Ichinomiya.

A cast-bronze lantern (karakane toro) of Nukisaki Shrine, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1997-04-23. Located at 1535 Ichinomiya.

name_en: Register of the Dai-dai Kagura at Ichinomiya Nukiboko Shrine
description_en: A membership register (gorenmeiroku) for the dai-dai kagura sacred dance at Ichinomiya Nukiboko Shrine, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1997-04-23. On deposit at the municipal art museum, 351-1 Kurokawa.
The great ginkgo tree (icho) of Chogakuji temple, designated as a natural monument. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1998-06-11. Located at 698 Kamitakao.

A stone seated statue of a Nyorai triad, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1998-06-11. Located at Harada Shino public hall, 125 Tashino.

A stone tablet (itabi) bearing an inscription dated Kenji 2, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1999-06-11. Located at Ryukoji cemetery, 1193 Tomioka.

A stone tablet (itabi) bearing an inscription dated Gen'o 2, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1999-06-11. Located at 777 Okamoto.

A stone tablet (itabi) bearing an inscription dated Joji 2, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1999-06-11. Located at 777 Okamoto.

A haiku monument (kuhi) commemorating Basho at Fujiki, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1999-06-11. Located within the Kannon hall precinct, 249 Fujiki.
Ichinomiya burial mound No. 4, designated as a historic site. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 2001-04-13. Located at 343-2, the prefectural joint government building.
The great ginkgo tree (icho) of Wago, designated as a natural monument. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 2001-04-13. Located at 399-2 Tajima.
The great cedar tree (osugi) of Azuma Shrine, designated as a natural monument. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 2002-03-25. Located at 963-ko Moroto, Myogimachi.

name_romaji: Nanokaichi Hanshu Maeda-ke no Bosho・Maeda-ke Kankei Ihai, Kakochō, Hanshu Mokuzō
name_en: Grave Site of the Maeda Lords of Nanokaichi Domain with Mortuary Tablets, Death Register and Lords' Statues
description_en: The grave site of the Maeda lords of Nanokaichi domain, with related mortuary tablets, death register and wooden statues of the lords, designated as a historic site. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 2002-04-12. Located at 699 and 711-1 Kamitakao.

name_romaji: Maeda Toshitaka Seibo Myōun-ni no Haka Ihai
name_en: Tomb and Mortuary Tablet of Myoun-ni, Birth Mother of Maeda Toshitaka
description_en: The tomb and mortuary tablet (ihai) of Myoun-ni, birth mother of Maeda Toshitaka, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 2002-04-12. Located at 883 Nanokaichi-otsu and 884 Nanokaichi.

name_en: Maeda Family Treasure Pagoda (Nanokaichi Burial Mound No. 7)
description_en: The Maeda family treasure pagoda, situated at Nanokaichi burial mound No. 7, designated as a historic site. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 2002-04-12. Located at 1451 Nanokaichi.

name_romaji: Tomioka Seishijō Kōjo Ra no Haka (Kaigenji)
name_en: Graves of Tomioka Silk Mill Workers (Kaigenji)
description_en: The graves of factory workers of the Tomioka Silk Mill, at Kaigenji, designated as a historic site. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 2004-01-09. Located at 151 Tomioka.

name_romaji: Tomioka Seishijō Kōjo Ra no Haka (Ryūkōji)
name_en: Graves of Tomioka Silk Mill Workers (Ryukoji)
description_en: The graves of factory workers of the Tomioka Silk Mill, at Ryukoji, designated as a historic site. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 2004-01-09. Located at 1193 Tomioka.

name_romaji: Yūten Myōgō Kakejiku (Eishinji Zō) Tsuketari Riken Myōgō Kakejiku
name_en: Yuten Invocation Hanging Scroll (Eishinji Collection) with Sword-Invocation Hanging Scroll
description_en: A hanging scroll bearing a Yuten invocation held at Eishinji, with an attached sword-invocation hanging scroll, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 2004-01-09. Located at 884 Nanokaichi.

name_en: Yuten Invocation Hanging Scroll (Jingu Family Collection)
description_en: A hanging scroll bearing a Yuten invocation held by the Jingu family, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 2004-01-09. Located at 554 Uda.

name_en: Invocation Stone Pillar of Hoshida Kokuzo
description_en: An invocation stone pillar (myogoto) at Hoshida Kokuzo, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 2004-01-09. Located within the precinct at 1012-2 Hoshida.
The holly tree (hiiragi) of the Kijima family, designated as a natural monument. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 2005-03-24. Located at 41 Furutate, Myogimachi.

name_en: Large Votive Tablet of Fushimi Shrine
description_en: A large votive picture tablet (oema) of Fushimi Shrine, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 2005-07-19. Located at 1312 Shimotakada, Myogimachi.
The great ginkgo tree (icho) of Ryukoji temple, designated as a natural monument. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 2007-02-02. Located at 1193 Tomioka.

name_en: Former Kozuke Railway Onigasawa Iron Bridge
description_en: The former Kozuke Railway Onigasawa iron bridge, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 2007-09-29. Located at Kosakazaka, Najai.

The Deki-type electric locomotive No. 2, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 2007-09-29. On outdoor display at the municipal art museum, 1377-1 Kamikuroiwa.
A seated statue of Fudo Myoo carved by Enku, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 2008-03-28. Located at 6 Myogi, Myogimachi.
The mukuroji (soapberry) tree of Ugei Shrine, designated as a natural monument. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 2010-04-28. Located at 1178 Kannari.

The Oshima one-hundred-and-eight lantern observance (hyakuhatto), classified as a folk cultural property. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 2011-06-27. Located at 167-1 and 168-1 Oshima.

name_romaji: Kanrasha Bankoku Hakurankai Shōjō Tsuketari Shōhai
name_en: Kanrasha World Exposition Certificate with Medal
description_en: The Kanrasha world exposition award certificate, with an attached medal, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 2013-06-27. Held at the municipal art museum, 351-1 Kurokawa, Tomioka City.
Miyazaki Park, designated as a place of scenic beauty. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 2021-03-26. Located at 329-1 Miyazaki.

name_en: Raiden Shrine (Auxiliary Hachimangu and Inari Shrine Halls)
description_en: The shrine halls of the auxiliary Hachimangu and Inari shrines at Raiden Shrine, classified as early-modern structures under tangible cultural properties. Town-designated by Itakura, Gunma Prefecture.

name_en: Main Hall Carvings of Raiden Shrine
description_en: The main-hall (honden) carvings of Raiden Shrine, classified as early-modern structures under tangible cultural properties. Town-designated by Itakura, Gunma Prefecture.
